source_text:
8. "Via:" Line

a. General. Use a "Via:" line when one or more activities outside of your activity should review a letter before it reaches the action addressee. The format for the "Via:" line is the same as for the "From:" line and "To:" line discussed in paragraphs 5 and 6 above.

b. Format. Type "Via:" at the left margin on the first line below the "To:" line. Five spaces follow the colon. If the entry is longer than one line, start the second line under the first word after the heading.

EXAMPLE:
From: Chief of Naval Operations To: Commander, U.S. Fleet Forces Command Via: Commander, U.S. Pacific Fleet

c. Numbering Via Addressees. Number "Via" addressees if two or more are listed. Follow the chain of command. Routing starts with the addressee listed first.

EXAMPLE:
From: Commander, Destroyer Squadron 23 To: Commander, U.S. Fleet Forces Command Via: (1) Commander, Carrier Strike Group NINE
(2) Commander, U.S. THIRD Fleet (3) Commander, Naval Surface Force, U.S. Pacific Fleet (4) Commander, U.S. Pacific Fleet

d. When one or more activities outside of your activity should review a letter before it reaches the action addressee, and TV-5 Taskers is the mechanism being used for routing the correspondence, do not use a "Via:" line. Prepare the appropriate Action Memorandum as outlined on page 12-4 of this manual.
plain_language_rule: The Via line is used when one or more activities outside the originator's activity must review a letter before it reaches the action addressee. The Via label is typed at the left margin on the first line directly below the To line, followed by five spaces before the addressee information. If the entry extends beyond one line, continuation lines align under the first word after the heading. When two or more Via addressees are listed, number them sequentially following the chain of command, with routing starting from the first-listed addressee. When TV-5 Taskers is used for routing correspondence, do not use a Via line; instead prepare an Action Memorandum per page 12-4.
renderer_impact: Header section Via line rendering (optional); placement on first line below To line (no spacing gap); five-space offset after colon; left-margin alignment; continuation line alignment under first word after heading; numbered Via addressees when multiple (sequential numbering per chain of command); TV-5 Taskers exception handling (no Via line when using Action Memorandum routing)
notes: CRITICAL PLACEMENT RULE: Via line is on the FIRST line under To line - no blank line between To and Via. Five spaces follow the colon (vs. two spaces for From line, six spaces for To line). Manual explicitly states format is the same as From/To lines for continuation line alignment (second line under first word after heading). Multiple Via addressees must be numbered sequentially (1), (2), (3), etc. following chain of command order. Routing hierarchy starts with first-listed Via addressee. TV-5 Taskers is an exception case - when used for routing, do not include Via line; use Action Memorandum per page 12-4 instead. RELATIONSHIP TO OTHER SECTIONS: Via line is optional header element used for routed correspondence requiring intermediate review before reaching action addressee. Chapter 9 (Endorsements) governs Via addressee behavior - Via addressees must forward with endorsement (may be simple 'forwarded' when no opinion needed). Via addressees may take final action, divert routing, or return to originator with explanation. AMBIGUITY: Manual does not explicitly specify exact baseline spacing between To and Via lines beyond 'first line below' - should follow same pattern as From→To (1 leading unit, 14.4 pt). DO NOT activate until renderer geometry confirms Via line placement at To_baseline + 1 leading unit.
